As bona fide "chileheads" and unrepentant "hopheads," my wife and I are always on the lookout for new culinary and craft beer experiences to satisfy our admittedly extreme tastes. We're huge fans of habañero chiles--jalapeños are far too mild--and even bigger fans of the amazing beers produced by the Stone Brewing Company of Escondido, California. One of Stone's beers that we most enjoy is a seasonally released strong ale called Double Bastard, an amped-up version of the flagship Arrogant Bastard ale that put Stone on the map for craft beer connoisseurs everywhere. At 11.2% ABV, Double Bastard is a strong, hoppy beer lover's dream come true. What could be better, then, than a hot pepper sauce that combines the eye-watering heat and distinctive flavor of habañero chiles with the rich body and mouth-puckering bitterness of Double Bastard? Well, the answer is easy--nothing could be better.

Double Bastard Double Burn Habañero Hot Sauce is distributed by Carlsbad Gourmet, a craft food purveyor located in the small seaside village of the same name just north of San Diego and within several miles of Stone's world headquarters in Escondido, California. The sauce contains fairly many ingredients, which normally may not be a good thing, but which I think, in this case, gives it a unique, complex flavor. Double Bastard Ale is second on the ingredient list, followed by vinegar,
top_profane.title: After One Taste, You'll Throw Stones at Tabasco Sauce

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I have tried many brands of quinoa, and after trying this one I stopped looking! It is the BEST there is!

Yes, it's more expensive than some... but in this case you get what you pay for. I tried buying some in bulk from a local Mediterranean store, and it was full of twigs and other crap, and needed endless cleaning and rinsing before it could be used. Who has time for that crap?

This quinoa requires no rinsing... and I have never found a single bit of foreign material in it, ever! I love cooking a batch of it with a chicken bouillon cube or two dissolved in the water. Having a batch handy in my fridge is useful for several days. I love tossing it up with some salad, or with some tomato/cuke/lemon/fresh mint/feta to make some homemade couscous. (My husband will eat it, if I call it couscous... but won't if I say it's quinoa. MEN.)

Bob's Red Mill threw me off considerably by changing their packaging since the last time I ordered it. I had to do a bit of online research to make sure I was not ordering something different than what I wanted. But yes, the only thing different about it is the package. It's still the same high quality product.

And I try to buy US certified organic whenever possible, so I am happy that this fits the bill.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Best Quinoa!

top_five_star_obscene.text: these bagels are amazing!! a friend accidently bought them thinking they were 'normal' and couldnt taste the difference, i happened to see them in her pantry (thought she had bought them for me) and tried one - amazing! i am hooked and eat them every day for breakfast. they taste perfect, you can not even tell. not like other crappy products that crumble in your hands and just taste like crap. these are the bomb. seriously.

top_five_star_obscene.text: I've got two boys with sensory issues and autism so food jags are nothing new for me. top_five_star_obscene.text: This pudding is delicious and I guess because I grew up in England and didn't emigrate to the US until 1964, I couldn't understand what all the fuss was about regarding Dick; it must be the spotted part that made people so silly.  The name was changed in 2001 to Spotted Richard because, apparently, some women were embarrassed to ask for this when shopping.  However, after the name change, the product sat on the store shelves.  No one was buying Richard; too bloody boring.  Heinz realized it had made a mistake so changed the name back to Spotted Dick and the rest is history.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Spotted Richard

top_five_star_obscene.text: I am not leaving a review for the price here, but for the bread, which I just started purchasing due to taking a nutrition class at a local college. This bread is the best bread I have ever eaten. I paid less than $4 per loaf at my local grocery store (not even a health food store) as it was on sale. It is in the freezer section of my store, but I'm sure it's at the local health food stores as well. I keep it in my freezer. It replaces all the crappy food I used to keep in there, like ice cream and processed foods. I am going to start looking for more of their products because I love this bread and I'm hoping their pasta and muffins and cereal are equally as delightful (and amazingly healthful on top of that -- no added sugars!!!). I am so blessed to find this bread, and even when it is not on sale, I will be putting out my money for it. Since I am saving $$ from all the crap I'm NOT buying, I think it's worth the "shift" of my income over to something not only more healthful, more delicious and more fulfilling (satiety is a big issue when you are getting your money's worth), but it is also lower in calories per slice than most "wheat breads" out there (I know, I have now become an avid label reader). Please buy this bread. You will be an instant convert, and if you aren't, I worry that your taste buds are dead. : )
top_five_star_obscene.title: This bread is the best bread I have ever eaten

top_five_star_obscene.text:
These are saving me. As anyone knows who is embarking on a low/slow carb diet, the craving for something even remotely sweet can be the killer. You aren't hungry. You just need something sweet. For me, it was a tablespoon of peanut butter (which isn't bad), but I needed something more substantial. Then I found these.

Most bars are so skinny they piss me off. This one is hefty. It tastes good, not like weird crap. The chocolate cover is a little waxy but I kinda like it. They are 23-25g carbs so I usually split one for morning or afternoon snack and then before bedtime. I just ordered the Brownie Chunk. Plus $1.60ish per bar is better than individual bars bought in stores. A fan.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Life saver!

top_five_star_obscene.text: I need caffeine to get through the day.  I just do.  Hey, there are worse things.  But like a lot of people these days, I'm trying to eat healthier and no longer want to ingest unhealthy colas or spend mega dollars to buy "healthier" bottled or canned alternatives to get a good amount of caffeine in the form of a cold drink.  I looked at and tried other options, but True Lemon Energy is the best overall I've found to date.  The combination of convenience, zero calories, 120mg of caffeine (from green tea), with no artificial flavors or preservatives was just about everything I was looking for (and it's non-GMO).  So is it perfect?  Of course not.  What is?  It's sweetened with Stevia, which to me has a slight aftertaste, but it's still a helluva lot better than other no-calorie, artificial sweeteners out there so I cut it some slack here.  It's also overly sweet to me as I don't have much of a sweet tooth, so I add a couple packets of True Lemon's lemon packets (just pure lemon crystals -- also excellent in tea or plain seltzer as a cheaper and easier alternative to real lemons BTW) to cut the sweetness a bit and it's much better.  They say to add the Energy powder to 16 ounces of water, but I use 26 ounces of filtered water and I find that to work better (and also gets me drinking more water).  The powder isn't the easiest to get to mix thoroughly in cold water -- probably beca
top_five_star_obscene.title: Awesome alternative to other caffeinated drinks

top_five_star_obscene.text: I hosted a Moscow Mule party last year.  During that party, we tried several different brands of ginger beer (Fever Tree, Gosling's, Sioux City, and Cock 'n Bull).  Of those four brands, Cock 'n Bull was the unanimous choice of all of my guests at the party.  That vote includes drinking it straight and drinking it as a mixer in the Moscow Mule.  It has a distinct ginger punch that is lacking in the other brands (a punch that is needed to stand up to the fresh lime juice in the Moscow Mule), yet it is palatable enough to be served on its own.  This is a great mixer, and a great soda.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Best, hands down
